utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. Now check each in the final plan: 1) health_coverage: The format requires 'physical and mental health insurance with costs.' The plan lists physical_health and mental_health plan names and separate costs for each. That satisfies the required item with clear costs. 2) property_coverage: The format requires 'home/contents insurance with costs.' The plan lists home_contents_insurance with a cost. That satisfies the item. 3) travel_coverage: The format requires 'travel insurance with costs.' The plan lists a travel insurance plan and a cost. That satisfies the item. 4) additional_services: The format requires 'any enhancement services with costs.' The plan lists adventure_travel_coverage with a cost, and premium_insurance_membership with a cost; also includes a discount detail. Costs are provided, so it satisfies the item. 5) total_budget: The format requires 'complete monthly/annual cost breakdown.' The plan provides only a monthly total (no annual). Because it lacks the annual breakdown, it is not fully specified according to the required details. Therefore, 4 items are fully specified out of 5.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes specific products: PrimeCare Premium Plan (for both physical and mental health), PrimeHome Premium Coverage (home contents), PrimeTravel Premium Plan (travel), Adventure Travel Coverage, and Premium Insurance Membership. I matched each of these to the corresponding entries in the ground-truth ratings and extracted their exact ratings.
